segments.s759-g17: Azarm here is used as an abstract noun (an older/poetic word for modesty/shame) governed by the preposition از used in the causal sense ‘because of/with’. گیتی (the world) is the subject and بی‌آزرم is an adjective formed with the prefix بی- ‘without/…less’ meaning ‘shameless’; گشت (past of گشتن) functions as a stative verb ‘became’. Word order is emphatic: causal prepositional phrase → subject → predicate.
segments.s759-g18: پی here means ‘after/following’ and governs the noun اختر ‘star’. رفتنش is the verbal noun (رفتن + possessive/nominalizing ـش) meaning ‘his/its going’ or ‘departure’. نرم is an adverbial/ adjectival comment ‘gentle(ly)’, and گشت is again the past/stative ‘became’. The clause literally reads ‘Following the star, his going became gentle.’
segments.s759-g19: شد here means ‘happened’ (an intransitive past occurrence). اونیز (‘that also’ / ‘that too’) is emphatic; و simply links the next clause. آن تخت is the noun phrase ‘that throne’, and بی‌شاه is a predicate adjective ‘without a king/kingless’. ماند is the past of ماندن ‘remained/was left’ and completes the clause.
segments.s759-g20: به کام دل is an idiomatic prepositional phrase ‘to/for the desire of the heart’ (به = ‘to/for’, کام = ‘desire’, دل = ‘heart’), مَرْدِ بدخواه (‘mard-e badkhāh’) is a genitive/attributive group ‘the ill-wishing man’ (here: an enemy or malefactor). ماند again is the verb ‘remained/was left’, so the clause means ‘it remained to the wish of the ill-wisher.’
segments.s759-g21: همه کار is a nominal phrase ‘the whole affair/work’; گردنده چرخ literally ‘the turning/rotating wheel’ is a poetic image (گردنده modifies چرخ). این بود places the pointer ‘this was’ at the clause end, with بود as past copula. Word order is typical Persian: topical phrase → predicate → بود.
segments.s759-g22: ز is the poetic form of ‘from’/‘of’. پرورده is a passive/participial noun ‘what/whom he reared’ (nurtured). خوی + ش are split here: خوی is the base (kinship/disposition) and ش is the enclitic pronoun ‘his’, so پرورده خویش = ‘those he himself reared’. پرکین is a proper name; بود is the past copula ‘was’. The clause therefore states that Perkin was one of his own reared ones.
segments.s759-g9: هر آنکس که ... is a classical relative clause meaning "whoever/anyone who..."; هر انتخاب here has the sense 'any/whichever', آنکس is the head noun 'person', که introduces the clause, and باشد is the present/subjunctive form of 'to be' used in relative clauses ('is' / 'may be'). مرا ... دوستدار is an older word order: مرا (me) + دوستدار (literally 'fond of' / 'one who loves') expresses 'who loves me' (the object 'me' appears before دوستدار).
segments.s759-g10: چنانم ... is a compact clause meaning 'I will be thus/so' or 'such am I'; -م on چنانم marks the speaker. مر او را is an older sequence with the clitic/word مر (here an irregular/pronominal form not fully matched in the given senses) plus او را ('to/for him' with the object marker را); چو is the comparative 'like/as', and پروردگار is the standard noun 'Lord / nurturer'. The whole gives 'I will be to him like a Lord.'
segments.s759-g11: کس کو ز پیمان من بگذرد is a conditional/relative phrase 'whoever/anyone who departs from my covenant'. کس = 'anyone/someone'; کو here is an older/pronominal/relative element attached to کس (the provided senses don't list a 'who' sense, so it is an archaic relative use); ز = 'from'; پیمان من = 'my covenant/pledge'; بگذرد is a subjunctive/relative use of گذشتن 'to pass/overstep' meaning 'to violate/leave'.
segments.s759-g12: بپیچید ز آیین و راه خرد is the consequence/clause: بپیچید is an optative/jussive or imperative-form of پیچیدن meaning 'let him turn / may he be turned (away)'; ز = 'from'; آیین و راه خرد = 'the custom/rite and the way of wisdom' (آیین here = 'custom/practice' and خرد = 'wisdom'). The verb is commanding or predicting a turning-away as the result of breaking the covenant.
segments.s759-g13: به خواری تنش را برآرم بدار is a compact threat: به خواری = 'to/into disgrace'; تنش را = 'his body' (تن + possessive ش + object marker را); برآرم is first-person singular (classical present/subjunctive or 'I will cause/raise') from برآوردن 'to raise/make', and بدار here denotes 'to the gallows / be hanged' (used as object/place), so the clause means 'I will put his body to shame and hanged him' or 'I will expose/humiliate him and have him hanged.'
segments.s759-g14: ز دهقان و تازی و رومی شمار uses ز ... شمار to mean 'count him among / reckon him with'. ز = 'among/from', دهقان، تازی، رومی are enumerated social/ethnic groups (here 'peasant/farmer', 'Arab', 'Rûm/Byzantine'), and شمار is the verb/noun 'count/tally' used idiomatically ('count him among ...').
segments.s759-g15: همی‌بود بر تخت بر چار ماه is a narrative-stative clause: همی‌بود is an archaic/poetic imperfective 'continued to be / was'; بر تخت = 'on the throne'; بر چار ماه uses the preposition بر before the duration 'چار ماه' ('for four months') — the repetition of بر is a poetic feature marking both location and duration.
segments.s759-g16: به پنجم شکست اندر آمد به گاه is a time-statement: به پنجم = 'on the fifth' (ordinal month implied), شکست = 'defeat' is the noun-subject, اندر آمد = 'came in/occurred' (اندر آمدنش = it came upon him), and به گاه is an adverbial 'at the time' — together meaning 'in the fifth (month) defeat came' / 'his defeat came in the fifth month.'
segments.s759-g1: یکی ... دیگر is an idiom meaning "one other / another." دخت (maiden/daughter) is the head noun; نام after a name marks it as "called/named." Here بد آزرم functions as a personal name (بد‑آزرم), so بد is not acting as the adjective "bad" but as part of the name construction.
segments.s759-g2: ز is the poetic form of از (from/by) indicating source or means. تاج بزرگان = "the crown of the great ones" (genitive). رسیده is a perfect/participial form "has reached/arrived," and به کام (idiom) means "to satisfaction / to fulfilment." Together: she has, by/through the crown of the nobles, reached satisfaction.
segments.s759-g3: بیامد is the simple past narrative "came." The prepositional phrase به تخت کیان (= "to the throne of the Kians/kings") indicates destination. برنشست is a compound past verb "sat upon (it)" — both verbs share the same omitted subject (the previously mentioned daughter).
segments.s759-g4: گرفت is past "took/seized." The doubled جهان (این جهان جهان را) is emphatic/poetic: این جهان (this world) then جهان را with را marks it as the definite object. به دست literally "into the hand," an idiom meaning to seize or bring under one's power (i.e., take control).
segments.s759-g5: نخستین چنین گفت is a formulaic narrative start: نخستین "first," چنین "thus/so," گفت here functions as "said" (verb) introducing reported speech. کای is a vocative "O," and بخردان is the vocative plural "wise ones / sages." Together: "First he said thus, 'O wise men...'"
segments.s759-g6: جهان گشته uses گشته (perfect/participle of شدن) to mean "the world has become/turned." و is the conjunction "and." کار کرده = "work/affairs done" (کرده as perfect participle). ردان is an agentive/qualifying word (here: "the rejecters/expellers"), so the phrase roughly states that the world has turned and the affairs have been carried out by those who reject/expel — the exact semantic role of ردان is poetically compressed but it modifies who has acted.
segments.s759-g7: همه کار = "all affairs." بر داد literally "upon justice" (داد = justice here), so the clause means "let us place all matters under justice." آیین کنیم uses کنیم (hortative/subjunctive "let us do") with آیین "custom/ordinance/ritual": "let us establish a practice/ordinance."
segments.s759-g8: کزین پس = "from this on / henceforth." همه خشت = "every brick" (خشت = mudbrick). بالین can mean "pillow" or "by/near," and here it is used poetically (literally "make every brick a pillow"), so کنیم is again hortative "let us make/do." The line is idiomatic/poetic rather than literal: it announces a change of practice from now on.

sentences.s759-s9: With modesty gone, the world became shameless; following the star, his going grew gentle.
sentences.s759-s10: That too happened, and that throne remained without a king; it remained to the desire of an ill-wisher.
sentences.s759-s11: This was the whole work of the turning wheel: Perkin was one of those he had reared.
sentences.s759-s5: Whoever is a friend of mine, I will be to him as a Lord.
sentences.s759-s6: Let anyone who breaks my covenant turn away from custom and the path of wisdom.
sentences.s759-s7: I will strip his body to disgrace and hang him; count him among peasant, Arab, and Roman.
sentences.s759-s8: He remained upon the throne for four months; in the fifth month his defeat occurred.
sentences.s759-s1: Another daughter, named Bad-Āzram, attained the crown of the great and fulfilled her desire.
sentences.s759-s2: She came to the Kian throne and sat upon it; she seized this world and took the world into her hand.
sentences.s759-s3: First he said thus, "O wise ones: the world has turned, and the affairs done by the rejecters..."
sentences.s759-s4: Let us put all affairs under justice and establish custom; henceforth let us make every brick a pillow.
